title: ImageEval 2026 — Ayn-VQA Task 1b: Hallucination Detection (MSA)
terms:
# Terms

## Submission format
Submit a **UTF-8 CSV** named `prediction.csv`, zipped as `prediction.zip`.
For every item, submit **three rows** — one per statement.

**Required columns**
- `id` — the item id (do not reorder/rename).
- `statement_index` — `0`, `1` or `2`, matching the position in `statements`.
- `prediction` — `true` if that statement is grounded in the image, else `false`.

```
id,statement_index,prediction
1dcdf6da...,0,false
1dcdf6da...,1,true
1dcdf6da...,2,false
```

## Rules
1. Your `id`s must exactly match the gold set for the active phase (`devtest`,
   then `test`); missing/extra rows are rejected.
2. Exactly one statement per item is grounded; predict `true`/`false` for all three.

## Metric
Ranking metric: **combined accuracy** — fraction of items where all three labels
are correct (the one grounded statement marked true, both hallucinated ones false).
The scorer also reports the hallucination rate and the True/False (Q+/Q−)
accuracies as secondary columns.

## Registration, data usage, ethics
- Please register at the **[participation form](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSd1QKF4rXD_gbLJlDykLvB0DGMIogwhraeOtWRiQiotucK0zA/viewform)**.
- Data is **CC BY-NC 4.0** (non-commercial research use only).
- Any pretrained model / external data / compute is allowed; document it in your paper.

## Contact
imageeval2026@gmail.com

pages.title: Participation
pages.content:
# How to participate

**ImageEval 2026 — Ayn-VQA Task 1b: Hallucination Detection (MSA)** — the **MSA** track of Task 1b
(Hallucination Detection), part of the [ImageEval 2026](https://imageeval2026.github.io/) shared task on cultural
grounding in Arabic multimodal generation and understanding.

Given an image and 3 statements (MSA), label EACH statement True (grounded in the image) or False (hallucinated). Exactly one statement is grounded.

## Register (please do this first)
Fill in the **[registration form](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSd1QKF4rXD_gbLJlDykLvB0DGMIogwhraeOtWRiQiotucK0zA/viewform)** — it lets the organisers track
participants and notify you about data, deadlines, and any updates.

## Who can join
Individuals or teams; one member submits on the team's behalf. You may enter more
than one track.

## Steps
1. Sign up on this Codabench page **and** the [registration form](https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSd1QKF4rXD_gbLJlDykLvB0DGMIogwhraeOtWRiQiotucK0zA/viewform).
2. Read the **Terms** tab — note the exact prediction format.
3. Get the data from the Hugging Face Hub: **[QCRI/AynVQA](https://huggingface.co/datasets/QCRI/AynVQA)**.
4. Follow the task description in the [tasks repo](https://github.com/ImageEval2026/ImageEval2026-tasks).
5. Predict on **devtest** during the development phase, and on **test** during the
   final-evaluation phase.

## System description paper
All teams are encouraged to submit a system-description paper for the workshop.

## Contact
imageeval2026@gmail.com

pages.title: Dataset
pages.content:
# Dataset

All splits live on the Hugging Face Hub — **[QCRI/AynVQA](https://huggingface.co/datasets/QCRI/AynVQA)** — as config
`task1b_msa`. This competition scores **devtest** (development phase) and **test**
(final phase).

```python
from datasets import load_dataset
ds = load_dataset("QCRI/AynVQA", "task1b_msa", split="devtest")
```

## Splits

| split | labels | items | phase | purpose |
|---|---|---:|---|---|
| `train`   | yes | 3000 | development | training / fine-tuning |
| `dev`     | yes | 500  | development | local validation |
| `devtest` | no  | 500  | development (leaderboard) | submission target, dev phase |
| `test`    | no  | 1000 | final evaluation | blind ranking (human-recorded audio) |

- **Language:** MSA.
- Media (images / audio) are referenced by **relative path** keyed on `id`.
- Task 1b is image + text only (no audio): three statements, exactly one grounded.

## Licence
CC BY-NC 4.0 — non-commercial research use only.

pages.title: Timeline
pages.content:
# Timeline

## Development phase
Tune on `train` + `dev`; submit predictions on **devtest** for the leaderboard.

**Window:** `2026-05-22 → 2026-07-19`  ·  up to 5 submissions/day, 100 total.

## Testing phase
The blind **test** set is released on [Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/datasets/QCRI/AynVQA); submit predictions
on `test` for the official ranking.

**Window:** `2026-07-20 → 2026-07-29`  ·  up to 20 submissions/day, 100 total.

> Dates are set in `competition.yaml` (`phases.start` / `phases.end`) — adjust if
> the schedule shifts. Announcements: [website](https://imageeval2026.github.io/).

phases.name: Development Phase
phases.description: Tune on train + dev; submit predictions on devtest for the leaderboard.

phases.name: Testing Phase
phases.description: Final blind evaluation on the held-out test split.
